paper_language: Consistent with the canonical intestinal stem cell differentiation axis, in which intestinal stem cells give rise to transit-amplifying progenitors and immature enterocyte states along the crypt axis; the specific "Immature Enterocytes 2" label is the atlas subcluster name rather than a literature-standard named edge.

Consistent with bidirectional epithelial-ILC crosstalk in inflamed intestinal mucosa, where epithelial cells activate ILC programs via alarmins and ILCs reciprocally regulate progenitor differentiation; the directed CFN edge should be described as a co-regulatory relationship rather than a one-way causal claim.

Consistent with epithelial-mesenchymal niche feedback in the intestinal crypt, where subepithelial myofibroblasts regulate progenitor behavior through WNT, BMP, and Notch signaling; the reverse progenitor-to-myofibroblast direction is inferred from reciprocal niche biology rather than directly demonstrated as a named edge.

Consistent with atlas-level UC interaction rewiring in which inflammation-associated fibroblast programs and disease-expanded CD8+ IL-17+ T-cell states are jointly implicated; this should be framed as atlas-consistent co-occurrence and interaction-network relevance, not as a directly validated RSPO3-to-IEL mechanistic pathway.

Plausible as a co-occurrence of epithelial remodeling states in injured mucosa, but the mechanistic link between Tuft cells and TA progenitors is not strong enough for the main structural claim without a direct supporting citation.

paper_language: Consistent with the same epithelial-mesenchymal niche biology as the donor-global progenitor-myofibroblast edge, but the LP/Epi prefixes arise from the blocked benchmark representation and should not be overread as direct proof of literal compartment-to-compartment causality.
Plausible as an immune-stromal interaction in the lamina propria, but the specific Treg-to-WNT2B+ Fos-hi edge does not yet have direct literature support and should stay secondary.

paper_language: CD8+ IL-17+ T-cell expansion in UC is supported by the Smillie atlas, and co-occurrence with epithelial remodeling is plausible, but a direct CD8+ IL17-to-Tuft mechanistic edge is not established strongly enough for the main interpretation.

Exclude from paper figures and biological interpretation: MT-high states are standard scRNA cautionary signals consistent with stressed or damaged cells rather than a stable interpretable biological program.
Exclude from paper figures and biological interpretation for the same MT-high technical-caution reason as the endothelial row.
Co-occurrence of plasma cells and endothelial states in inflamed UC tissue is plausible, but edge direction and mechanism are unclear, so this should remain low-priority supplementary material.
